title: oplismenus setarius
intro: Oplismenus setarius, also known as the false brome or Oplismenus grass, is a perennial grass native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, and is also believed to have antibacterial properties that aid in wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
